typical_relief_triggers: Humorous character animations and parody elements
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Round-based arcade fighting with comedic timing breaks; tension builds during individual matches, relieved by round transitions and humorous clay-animated character interactions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory arcade opponents; basic move tutorials implied through early matchups
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased opponent AI aggression and special move frequency; tournament bracket progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters with maximum special move usage; final tournament opponents

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential opponent matches building toward championship; standard fighting game arcade mode structure
Humorous character designs that parody Street Fighter and Mortal Kombat conventions; comedic visual design carries gameplay pacing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any standard match featuring recurring cast (Bad Mr. Frosty, Blob, Bonker, Taffy)

notable_level_patterns.description: Victory animations and character movement rendered from clay-animated figures; visual humor breaks tension between competitive rounds

extraction_notes.data_limitations: Wikipedia source provides character roster and game release information but minimal level design sequencing, spatial structure, or detailed rhythm/pacing documentation. Fighting game paradigm inferred from genre classification (fighting/beat 'em up). Tier T3 (empirical/qualitative) limits precision on quantifiable metrics.
